entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: A legume crop (Cicer arietinum) whose rhizobia populations were studied.

entities.name: RHIZOBIUM
entities.description: A genus of bacteria that nodulates chickpea and fixes nitrogen.

entities.name: PLANT INFECTION DILUTION METHOD
entities.description: A method developed at ICRISAT for counting chickpea rhizobia.

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: The institution where the plant infection dilution method was developed.

entities.name: ALFISOLS
entities.description: A soil type used to study chickpea rhizobia populations.

entities.name: VERTISOLS
entities.description: A soil type where rhizobia populations were studied and found to decrease with depth.

entities.name: ENTISOLS
entities.description: A soil type used to study chickpea rhizobia populations.

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: An ICRISAT mandate crop used in pot culture to test rhizobia multiplication.

entities.name: MILLET
entities.description: An ICRISAT mandate crop used in pot culture to test rhizobia multiplication.

entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: An ICRISAT mandate crop used in pot culture to test rhizobia multiplication.

entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: An ICRISAT mandate crop used in pot culture to test rhizobia multiplication.

entities.name: PADDY
entities.description: A crop that, when following chickpea, causes a decrease in rhizobia population.

entities.name: POT CULTURE
entities.description: A method used to compare the stimulatory effect of different crops on rhizobia multiplication.

entities.name: SOIL DEPTH
entities.description: The depth of the soil, which affects the population of rhizobia in Vertisols.
